summary: Complete the MCP dispatcher tools/list result with the ten application-owned tool definitions instead of an empty placeholder list.

decisions.decision: Return all ten named tool definitions from tools/list with application-owned descriptions and closed base object schemas; leave detailed per-tool schema refinement to the server acceptance unit.
decisions.rationale: An empty tools/list response would be a false MCP capability surface. Metadata now accurately advertises the contract foundation without claiming handlers or stdio readiness.

testEvidence.criterion: tools/list returns named definitions and MCP contract/dispatcher tests remain green.
testEvidence.test: Run the focused MCP contract and dispatcher suites once after replacing the empty metadata result.

red.outcome: RED: the dispatcher returned an empty tools list and the new definition assertion failed.

green.outcome: GREEN: Test Files 2 passed (2); Tests 5 passed (5); Duration 430ms.

testEvidence.criterion: The metadata refinement remains type-safe and source-policy clean.
testEvidence.test: Run TypeScript and repository lint/source policy once after the bounded correction.

red.outcome: RED: the metadata refinement had not yet been applied.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false; pnpm.cmd lint
green.outcome: GREEN: TypeScript exited 0; Checked 435 files in 218ms with source policy passed.

questions: Detailed Appendix B per-tool input/output schemas, stdio framing, initialize handshake, and desktop handler wiring remain pending.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
